primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign currency risk arising from intercompany dividend receivables can be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 hedge accounting requirements

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 6.3.5 establishes the general requirement that hedged items must be with external parties, while paragraph 6.3.6 provides the specific exception for intragroup monetary items' foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies the scope of hedged risk that can be designated for foreign currency exposure in net investment hedges and addresses functional currency differences in group structures

references: IAS 21 paragraph as referenced in IFRS 9 6.3.6
supporting_authority.reason: Governs when foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items are not fully eliminated on consolidation based on different functional currencies

peripheral_authority.reason: Disclosure requirements for hedge accounting but do not govern the eligibility of hedged items
Addresses classification of dividends as expense versus equity distribution but not hedge accounting eligibility
Addresses measurement and recognition of dividend payables but not hedge accounting treatment

treatment_families.family: Intragroup monetary item foreign currency risk hedge

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item foreign currency risk hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 explicitly permits designation of foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items (including receivables between subsidiaries) as hedged items in consolidated financial statements when functional currencies differ and FX gains/losses affect consolidated P&L

approaches.label: Intragroup forecast transaction hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 generally prohibits designation of intragroup items as hedged items unless they qualify under the paragraph 6.3.6 exception, and paragraph 6.3.3 requires forecast transactions to be highly probable with external parties
